reason: The image shows a large ship passing through a canal lock, with a group of workers standing nearby. The workers are wearing clothing appropriate for the early 20th century, including overalls and hats. The architecture of the lock walls seems plausible for the Panama Canal era. The general atmosphere of the image aligns with the historical context. However, the ship in the image resembles the Titanic, which sank in 1912 and therefore would not have been passing through the Panama Canal in 1914. Additionally, the concrete block size of the lock walls seems somewhat large and uniformly square which may suggest a modern fabrication method, even though block construction was used at the time.
